title: Incorrect Stack Implementation: Empty Pop Operation

statement.markdown: Design a stack that supports insert, remove, pop operations, it should support O(1) time complexity for insert and remove operations and O(n) time complexity for pop which removes the top element. Your implementation should support an additional operation called `peek` which returns the top element without removing it.
statement.inputFormat: Input format is an array of integers. Each integer represents a new element to be pushed onto the stack.
statement.outputFormat: Output format is a single integer which is the top element on the stack after executing all the push/pop operations.
statement.notes: You may assume that all the elements are non-negative integers.

examples.explanation: Push 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, then pop 5, the top element is 5

hints.text: Implement the push/pop/peek operations with O(1)/O(n)/O(1) time complexity respectively

editorial.title: Stack Implementation
editorial.intuition: Use a custom made stack class to implement the operations with the required time complexities
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a linked list to implement the stack where each node represents a stack element, use a pointer to keep track of the top element and another pointer to keep track of the last element
commonMistakes: Not considering the case where the stack is empty during the pop/peek operation

starterCode.python:
class Solution:
  def __init__(self): pass
  def push(self, x): pass
  def pop(self): pass
  def peek(self): pass
  def isEmpty(self): pass
